sourceMovie.title: American Hustle
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit American Hustle for the same reason it broke through in 2013: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. David O. Russell keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Wolf of Wall Street
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If American Hustle clicked for you, The Wolf of Wall Street (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Catch Me If You Can
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If American Hustle clicked for you, Catch Me If You Can (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and crime, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after American Hustle?
faq.answer: Try The Wolf of Wall Street, Catch Me If You Can, Casino—each shares DNA with American Hustle while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is American Hustle?
faq.answer: American Hustle (2013) is best described as Drama, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is American Hustle worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
